steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to “emphasize a difference” between Vulpes zerda and Sterkiella histriomuscorum. The notes provide the key contrast: Vulpes zerda has 64 chromosomes while Sterkiella histriomuscorum has over 15,000. A correct choice must compare both organisms and highlight that gap.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Sterkiella histriomuscorum has over 15,000 chromosomes, which is far more than the sixty-four chromosome Vulpes zerda has.': Directly names both organisms, provides both chromosome counts, and uses “far more than” to highlight the difference. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Sterkiella histriomuscorum, a simple, single celled protozoan, has over 15,000 cellular structures called chromosomes.': Describes only Sterkiella; never mentions Vulpes zerda, so no comparison is made. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Sterkiella histriomuscorum and Vulpes zerda both have chromosomes, which are cellular structures that contain genes.': Emphasizes what the two organisms share, not a difference between them. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Individual members of the fennec fox species typically have the same number of chromosomes: sixty-four.': Addresses only Vulpes zerda and says nothing about Sterkiella. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
